prompt:
Our maker-space runs a puzzle night and I want a Nim referee for the kiosk: it enforces the rules, plays perfectly when someone hits the 'bot' button, and — this matters — plays IDENTICALLY on every kiosk, because we print the game logs and people compare them. Implement the engine in this crate (rust-nim) as src/lib.rs. The reviewed acceptance tests are in tests/nim.rs; don't touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore. Stdlib only.

Rules of play: heaps of counters, two players (0 and 1, player 0 always moves first), a move takes 1..=n counters from exactly one heap. Two variants, pub enum Variant { Normal, Misere } (derive Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq): in Normal the player who takes the last counter WINS, in Misere that player LOSES.

API: pub struct Game with:
- Game::new(heaps: &[u32], variant: Variant) -> Result<Game, String> — reject an empty slice ("need at least one heap") and all-zero heaps ("need at least one object"); zero-sized heaps among non-zero ones are fine.
- heaps() -> &[u32], turn() -> u8, variant() -> Variant, is_over() -> bool (all heaps empty).
- apply(&mut self, heap: usize, take: u32) -> Result<(), String>. Reject, changing nothing, exactly these: after the game ended "game over"; bad index "no such heap: 3"; take of zero "must take at least one"; overdraw "heap 1 has only 4" (also covers empty heaps: "heap 0 has only 0"). A good move removes the counters and flips the turn.
- winner() -> Option<u8> — None while running; once over, Normal gives it to the player who moved last, Misere to the other one.
- is_winning() -> bool — does the player to move win with optimal play? Normal play is the classic xor-sum criterion (winning iff the xor of all heap sizes is non-zero). Misere play uses the same criterion while at least one heap has 2+, but once EVERY heap is 0 or 1 it flips to parity: the mover wins iff the number of one-counter heaps is even. Returns false on a finished game.
- bot_move() -> Option<(usize, u32)> — None once over. Otherwise the bot's choice, deterministically: scan candidate moves by heap index ascending, and within a heap by removal count ascending, and return the FIRST move after which the opponent's position is losing (per the same criterion). If the current position is lost (no such move exists), the pinned fallback is to take exactly 1 from the lowest-index non-empty heap. Must not mutate the game.
- play_out(&mut self) -> Vec<(u8, usize, u32)> — both sides played by the bot until the game ends; each entry is (player, heap, take) in order.

The tests pin exact bot moves for a table of positions in both variants (e.g. normal [3,4,5] answers (0,2); misere [4] answers (0,3); misere and normal disagree on [2,1]), pin three complete play_out logs move for move, and then prove optimality wholesale: for a battery of fixture starts in both variants, if is_winning() claims the mover wins the bot must actually win the playout, and if it claims lost, the (optimal) opponent must win. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
